raw_consumer_statement: I filed a fraud claim in XX/XX/XXXX for XXXX unauthorized transactions totaling {$2600.00} on my Barclaycard XXXX XXXX. After initially denying the claim, Barclays eventually issued partial credits, and after CFPB intervention, further credits were applied. However, their latest response on XX/XX/XXXX, still fails to clearly match those credits to the original disputed transactionsspecifically, XXXX {$500.00} charges at XXXX # XXXX and XXXX # XXXX from XX/XX/XXXX remain unaccounted for. Barclays letter includes credits for other dates ( e.g., XX/XX/XXXX, XX/XX/XXXX, XX/XX/XXXX ) that do not correspond to the original transactions. I submitted a written follow-up on XX/XX/XXXX asking for a transaction-by-transaction reconciliation and documentation supporting their credit decisions. They never provided that. I am now requesting CFPBs help to compel full transparency and traceability between the original fraud charges and the credits issued.
